methods.description: Describes the setup and operation parameters for the LC-MS/MS system utilizing MRM mode for analyte detection and quantification, including instrument configuration, parameter settings, determination of optimal MRM conditions, and quantification using calibration curves.

steps.description: Mass spectrometry was performed on 5500 QTRAP (AB SCIEX) equipped with ESI source operated in negative mode.
steps.searchable_summary: Set up mass spectrometer for LC-MS/MS MRM analysis

steps.description: The parameters were as follows: capillary temperature, 550 °C; curtain gas, 35; ion spray voltage, −4500 V; ion source gas 1 (Gas 1), 55 psi; ion source gas 2 (Gas 2), 55 psi.
steps.searchable_summary: Configure mass spectrometer parameters for LC-MS/MS MRM analysis
inputs: Parameter values (capillary temperature, curtain gas, ion spray voltage, Gas 1, Gas 2)

steps.description: For each analyte the optimum conditions of MRM were determined in infusion mode are listed in Table 6.
steps.searchable_summary: Determine optimal MRM conditions for each analyte using infusion mode

steps.description: The quantification was done from the calibration curves obtained in MRM mode...
steps.searchable_summary: Obtain calibration curves from MRM data
inputs: MRM data from standards at different concentrations

steps.searchable_summary: Quantify analytes using calibration curves and Multi Quant software

methods.description: Describes the experimental procedures performed to validate the established LC-MS/MS (MRM) method according to specific guidance, including assessment of linearity, LODs, repeatability, stability, precision, accuracy (via recovery), and statistical analysis.

steps.description: The utilized validation concept was based on the guidance [H] GPH5-1 (CFDA, Beijing, China).
steps.searchable_summary: Adopt specific guidance for method validation

steps.description: Linearity, limits of detection (LODs), repeatability, stability, precision and accuracy were carried out under the developed method mentioned above.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform method validation procedures (Linearity, LODs, repeatability, stability, precision, accuracy)
outputs: Validation results (linearity data, LODs, repeatability data, stability data, precision data, accuracy data)

steps.description: The "intra-day" and the "inter-day" variances was intended to determine the precision.
steps.searchable_summary: Determine precision using intra-day and inter-day variance analysis
inputs: Analyte measurements from multiple runs/days using the established method

steps.description: A recovery experiment of analyte at three spiking levels was performed to investigate the accuracy.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform a recovery experiment at three spiking levels to assess accuracy

steps.description: A known amount of each of the 15 analytes at three concentrations of 75%, 100% and 125% was added to the corresponding FPT-N powder.
steps.searchable_summary: Spike sample matrix with known amounts of analytes at multiple concentrations

steps.description: Then the resultant samples were extracted...
...and analyzed with the established method in triplets at each level.
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze extracted spiked samples using the established LC-MS/MS (MRM) method in triplets

steps.description: The estimations of the RSD were based on the ANOVA.
steps.searchable_summary: Estimate Relative Standard Deviation (RSD) based on ANOVA for validation data
inputs: Analyte measurements from validation experiments (precision, accuracy)

steps.description: The data obtained from the LC-MS/MS were extracted and processed in the same way to permit an unbiased comparison.
steps.searchable_summary: Extract and process LC-MS/MS data consistently for unbiased comparison
inputs: Raw LC-MS/MS data from validation experiments
